devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अत्र सर्गो विसर्गश्च स्थानं पोषणमूतयः । मन्वन्तरेशानुकथा निरोधो मुक्तिराश्रयः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca atra sargo visargaśca sthānaṃ poṣaṇamūtayaḥ manvantareśānukathā nirodho muktirāśrayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
[1] Here (in the Bhāgavata Purāṇa) the (constituent) topics detailed are: (1) Subtle creation (sarga), (2) gross creation (visarga), (3) law and order (ensured by God) (sthāna), (4) protection—welfare of all (poṣaṇa), (5) material lust from Karmas (ūti), (6) the period of Manu and history thereof (manvantara), (7) accounts of God’s deeds (īśānukathā), (8) physical annihilation (nirodha), (9) liberation (mukti) and (10) the last resort of the universe, the ultimate Reality (āśraya).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The Sūta literature which, in the sacrificial milieu of the Brāhmaṇa period, became encyclopaedic, was classified under five topics which were later regarded as the characteristics of the compilations called the Purāṇas. Every Purāṇa thus consists of the following topics:
(1) Creation (sarga),
(2) recreation after dissolution (pratisarga),
(3) Genealogy of Gods and sages (vaṃśa),
(4) History of dynasties (vaṃśānucarita),
(5) Manu-periods of time (manvantara).
Purāṇas themselves, acknowledged these topics as forming their essential characteristics. Vide Agni-purāṇa 1.14, Garuḍa-purāṇa 1.2.27, Kūrma-purāṇa I, 1,12, Matsya-purāṇa 5.3.64, Śiva-purāṇa—Vāyavīya Saṃhitā 1.41, Vāyu-purāṇa—Prakrīyā Pāda 4.10.11; Viṣṇu-purāṇa 3.6.25. Under the influence of Vaiṣṇava theologies, these five topics were elaborated into ten topics enumerated in the above verses and later Bhāgavata-purāṇa 12.7.9-22 with a slight change in the nomenclature. As Balaḍeva Upādhyāya (Purāṇa-Vimarśa IV, pp. 125-139) shows there is not much difference between the contents of the five characteristics of Purāṇas in general and ten characteristics of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa

devanagari: दशमस्य विशुद्ध्यर्थं नवानामिह लक्षणम् । वर्णयन्ति महात्मानः श्रुतेनार्थेन चाञ्जसा ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: daśamasya viśuddhyarthaṃ navānāmiha lakṣaṇam varṇayanti mahātmānaḥ śrutenārthena cāñjasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ varṇ class 10 verb]

translation: For arriving at the accurate and real knowledge of the tenth characteristic (viz. the last resort of the universe or ultimate Reality) the great-souled persons describe the first nine topics by direct expression[2] (from the Vedas in eulogies etc.) and by way of purport (by episodes containing it).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
śrutenārthena—Bhāgavata Candrikā combines these words and explains ‘as actually described in authoritative books or Vedas’.

devanagari: भूतमात्रेन्द्रियधियां जन्म सर्ग उदाहृतः । ब्रह्मणो गुणवैषम्यात् विसर्गः पौरुषः स्मृतः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtamātrendriyadhiyāṃ janma sarga udāhṛtaḥ brahmaṇo guṇavaiṣamyāt visargaḥ pauruṣaḥ smṛtaḥ
translation: Due to the disturbance in the equilibrium of attributes, the production of the five elements (bhūtas), the objects of senses, the sense-organs and the intelligence (i.e, mahat, ahaṃkāra) is called the subtle creation. The gross creation produced by the Virāṭ Puruṣa is called Visarga

devanagari: स्थितिर्वैकुण्ठविजयः पोषणं तदनुग्रहः । मन्वन्तराणि सद्धर्म ऊतयः कर्मवासनाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sthitirvaikuṇṭhavijayaḥ poṣaṇaṃ tadanugrahaḥ manvantarāṇi saddharma ūtayaḥ karmavāsanāḥ
translation: Sthiti is the triumph of the Lord in the maintenance of (the divine) law and order (in everything), while the protection and welfare of all by his grace is Poṣaṇa. Manvantara consists of the account of the righteous path followed by Manu who observes his duty of protecting his subjects, while ūti is the desire for action (directed by) tendencies resulting from Karma.

devanagari: अवतारानुचरितं हरेश्चास्यानुवर्तिनाम् । पुंसां ईशकथाः प्रोक्ता नानाख्यान उपबृंहिताः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: avatārānucaritaṃ hareścāsyānuvartinām puṃsāṃ īśakathāḥ proktā nānākhyāna upabṛṃhitāḥ

translation: Īśānukathā is the description of the incarnations of Hari, and stories of the lives of his devotees with detailed stories.

devanagari: निरोधोऽस्यानुशयनं आत्मनः सह शक्तिभिः । मुक्तिः हित्वान्यथा रूपं स्वरूपेण व्यवस्थितिः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nirodho muktiḥ hitvānyathā rūpaṃ svarūpeṇa vyavasthitiḥ
translation: Nirodha is the withdrawal of the Jīva, along with all his powers and limitation in Hari when he enters his Yogic sleep. Mokṣa or liberation consists of abandoning the unreal form, and to stay in the essential nature of Brahman.

devanagari: आभासश्च निरोधश्च यतश्चाध्यवसीयते । स आश्रयः परं ब्रह्म परमात्मेति शब्द्यते ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: ābhāsaśca nirodhaśca yataścādhyavasīyate sa āśrayaḥ paraṃ brahma paramātmeti śabdyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: That from which creation and destruction are definitely known to emerge, is the resort which is called the Supreme Brahma, Supreme Soul, etc.

devanagari: योऽध्यात्मिकोऽयं पुरुषः सोऽसौ एवाधिदैविकः । यः तत्र उभय विच्छेदः स स्मृतोह्याधिभौतिकः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: yo yaḥ tatra ubhaya vicchedaḥ sa smṛtohyādhibhautikaḥ
translation: [3] He who regards organs of senses, such as eyes etc., as referring to himself is the seer or witness, the ādhyātmika Jīva. He is the same in the form of presiding deities of the senseorgans and is the ādhidaivika Jīva. He is the ādhibhautika (presiding over physical body) Jīva who is conditioned to see a distinction between the above two. (After the formation of the physical body, the two, viz. ādhyātmika and ādhidaivika, are separated.)
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī: Viṣṇu is the man who directs the organs of senses pertaining to the body (ādhyātmika). He is present also in the presiding deities of the sense organ (such as the Sun, the deity of perception or seeing). He is present in physical objects of perception, e.g. the jar etc. and the determiner between the ādhyātmika and ādhidaivika (e.g. the eye and the light seen by it). [This Viṣṇu is the āśraya (shelter) and controller of Jivas.]
According to Siddhāntapradīpa ādhyātmika is the set of cognitive and conative sense organs. Ādhidaivika is the group of presiding deities of the above organs. Physical body which is different from the above two is the ādhibhautika Puruṣa.

devanagari: एकं एकतराभावे यदा न उपलभामहे । त्रितयं तत्र यो वेद स आत्मा स्वाश्रयाश्रयः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ekaṃ ekatarābhāve yadā na upalabhāmahe tritayaṃ tatra yo veda sa ātmā svāśrayāśrayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: As we do not find (know) one in the absence of the other (i.e. if anyone of the three, viz. ādhyātmika, e.g. the eye and other organs, ādhidaivika (the presiding deity of the organ, e.g. the Sun or light), and the ādhibhautika, the physical sense organ, is absent, we cannot get any knowledge. (These are thus interdependent), he who knows all these three is the Soul who is not dependent on others, but is the support (shelter) of all.

devanagari: पुरुषोऽण्डं विनिर्भिद्य यदाऽसौ स विनिर्गतः । आत्मनोऽयनमन्विच्छन् अपः अस्राक्षीच्छुचिः शुचीः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣo ātmano

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ iṣ class 6 verb]

translation: [4] When this Virāṭ Puruṣa (Brahmā), bursting open the Cosmic Egg, came out and stood apart from the Brah'māṇḍa, he pondered over a place for himself. He himself being pure, created pure water (called garbhodaka).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Padaratnāvalī: Having created principles (tattvas), the Puruṣa entered the Cosmic Egg along with them. Having broken it open, he manifested himself as the same Puruṣa. He wished to have place for sleeping on the bed of Śeṣa (another transformation of his Self). Being himself eternally pure, he created pure water suitable to be a material worthy of use in his worship.

devanagari: तास्ववात्सीत् स्वसृष्टासु सहस्रं परिवत्सरान् । तेन नारायणो नाम यदापः पुरुषोद्‍भवाः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tāsvavātsīt svasṛṣṭāsu sahasraṃ parivatsarān tena nārāyaṇo nāma yadāpaḥ puruṣod‍bhavāḥ
translation: On those waters created by Him, he lay for a period of a thousand years. Waters were created by the Man (nara) [and hence came to be called nārā] He is called Nārāyaṇa (as nārā or waters were his ayana ‘place of abode’).

devanagari: द्रव्यं कर्म च कालश्च स्वभावो जीव एव च । यदनुग्रहतः सन्ति न सन्ति यद् उपेक्षया ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: dravyaṃ karma ca kālaśca svabhāvo jīva eva ca yadanugrahataḥ santi na santi yad upekṣayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: [5] It is by His grace that Matter, Action, Time, Innate nature and individual Soul (Jīva) function. They cease to do so when he becomes indifferent to them.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
cf. supra II. 5.14 and 22.

devanagari: एको नानात्वमन्विच्छन् योगतल्पात् समुत्थितः । वीर्यं हिरण्मयं देवो मायया व्यसृजत् त्रिधा ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: eko nānātvamanvicchan yogatalpāt samutthitaḥ vīryaṃ hiraṇmayaṃ devo māyayā vyasṛjat tridhā

[nominative single from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation: Getting up from his yogic bed of meditation, he desired to be many. Through his Māyā power, he divided his golden power (lit. gold-semen) in three parts—adhyātma adhidaiva and adhibhūta.

devanagari: अधिदैवं अथ अध्यात्मं अधिभूतमिति प्रभुः । अथैकं पौरुषं वीर्यं त्रिधा भिद्यत तच्छृणु ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: adhidaivaṃ atha adhyātmaṃ adhibhūtamiti prabhuḥ athaikaṃ pauruṣaṃ vīryaṃ tridhā bhidyata tacchṛṇu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation: Listen how the Lord differentiated one and the same energy (semen) of the (Cosmic) Man in three parts as adhidaiva, adhyātma and adhibhūta.

devanagari: अन्तः शरीर आकाशात् पुरुषस्य विचेष्टतः । ओजः सहो बलं जज्ञे ततः प्राणो महान् असुः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: antaḥ śarīra ākāśāt puruṣasya viceṣṭataḥ ojaḥ saho balaṃ jajñe tataḥ prāṇo mahān asuḥ

translation: From the ether (ākāśa) within the body of the Puruṣa who was making movements, were produced the powers of the senses, the mind and the body, and from these was produced (the subtle power of Prāṇa called Sūtra the chief Prāṇa (vital Power) of all.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā thinks that from the Prāṇa (vital energy) of god Brahmā, the Prāṇas of all beings were produced while Padaratnāvalī states that it is from Hari who was active within the interior cavity of the Virāṭ Puruṣa (or Brahmā) that the powers of Hari were produced, and these were the real source of the chief vital energy (Prāṇa), the possessor of the Powers called—sahas, ojas and bala.

devanagari: अनुप्राणन्ति यं प्राणाः प्राणन्तं सर्वजन्तुषु । अपानंतं अपानन्ति नरदेवं इवानुगाः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: anuprāṇanti yaṃ prāṇāḥ prāṇantaṃ sarvajantuṣu apānaṃtaṃ apānanti naradevaṃ ivānugāḥ
translation: All the organs of senses in living beings make movements when the chief Prāṇa is active. They stop their activities when he ceases to do so, like servants of a king following him.

devanagari: प्राणेन आक्षिपता क्षुत् तृड् अन्तरा जायते विभोः । पिपासतो जक्षतश्च प्राङ् मुखं निरभिद्यत ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇena ākṣipatā kṣut tṛḍ antarā jāyate vibhoḥ pipāsato jakṣataśca prāṅ mukhaṃ nirabhidyata

translation: By the activities of Prāṇa, hunger and thirst were aroused in the Lord (Virāṭ Puruṣa), When He desired to eat and drink, the mouth (face) first became separate (was formed) as a distinct organ of the body.

devanagari: मुखतः तालु निर्भिन्नं जिह्वा तत्र उपजायते । ततो नानारसो जज्ञे जिह्वया योऽधिगम्यते ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: mukhataḥ tālu nirbhinnaṃ jihvā tatra upajāyate tato nānāraso jajñe jihvayā yo

translation: [7] From the mouth, the palate became a separate member, and the tongue also was produced then. Then different kinds of tastes which are obtained (appreciated) by the tongue were produced.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
In this description of the members of the body of the Cosmic Man (Virāṭ Puruṣa), the group of four, viz. the place, (adhiṣṭhāna), the sense-organ, the object of the sense and the presiding deity, though not stated, are to be understood in these verses. For example, this verse indicates that the palate is the position or place, the tongue is the sense organ, different tastes are the objects of the sense, and Varuṇa is the presiding deity of this organ.

devanagari: विवक्षोर्मुखतो भूम्नो वह्निर्वाग् व्याहृतं तयोः । जले वै तस्य सुचिरं निरोधः समजायत ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: vivakṣormukhato bhūmno vahnirvāg vyāhṛtaṃ tayoḥ jale vai tasya suciraṃ nirodhaḥ samajāyata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: From the mouth of this great Being who desired to speak, were created the Fire (God agni, the presiding deity), the organ of speech and the speech which is under the control of the two (viz. the organ of speech and the presiding deity). Certainly, his breath was controlled in water for a long time (which led to the creation of nose for breathing).

devanagari: नासिके निरभिद्येतां दोधूयति नभस्वति । तत्र वायुः गन्धवहो घ्राणो नसि जिघृक्षतः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: nāsike nirabhidyetāṃ dodhūyati nabhasvati tatra vāyuḥ gandhavaho ghrāṇo nasi jighṛkṣataḥ

[ablative single from √ grah ], [genitive single from √ grah ]

translation: The two nostrils were formed, when the vital air began to move forcibly. When he (the Virāṭ Puruṣa) wished to have smell, the sense of smell was produced with Vāyu, the bearer of smell, as the presiding deity.

devanagari: यदाऽत्मनि निरालोकं आत्मानं च दिदृक्षतः । निर्भिन्ने ह्यक्षिणी तस्य ज्योतिः चक्षुः गुणग्रहः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā nirbhinne hyakṣiṇī tasya jyotiḥ cakṣuḥ guṇagrahaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ dṛś ], [ablative single from √ dṛś ], [genitive single from √ dṛś ]
[ablative single from √ dṛś ], [genitive single from √ dṛś ]

translation: Being desirous of seeing himself in the darkness within the body of the Virāṭ Puruṣa a pair of eyes was created along with light (tejas) and the organ of seeing, and the power of seeing (the light, forms, colours).

devanagari: बोध्यमानस्य ऋषिभिः आत्मनः तत् जिघृक्षतः । कर्णौ च निरभिद्येतां दिशः श्रोत्रं गुणग्रहः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: bodhyamānasya ṛṣibhiḥ ātmanaḥ tat jighṛkṣataḥ karṇau ca nirabhidyetāṃ diśaḥ śrotraṃ guṇagrahaḥ

[ablative single from √ grah ], [genitive single from √ grah ]

translation: When the Soul wished to hear the eulogies of the sages, two ears as well as the cardinal points (as presiding deities) and the auditory sense-organ were created. Hence the power of audition.

devanagari: वस्तुनो मृदुकाठिन्य लघुगुर्वोष्ण शीतताम् । जिघृक्षतः त्वङ् निर्भिन्ना तस्यां रोम महीरुहाः । तत्र चान्तर्बहिर्वातः त्वचा लब्धगुणो वृतः ॥...
english_devnagari: vastuno mṛdukāṭhinya laghugurvoṣṇa śītatām jighṛkṣataḥ tvaṅ nirbhinnā tasyāṃ roma mahīruhāḥ tatra cāntarbahirvātaḥ tvacā labdhaguṇo vṛtaḥ

[ablative single from √ grah ], [genitive single from √ grah ]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: When he wished to feel softness, hardness, lightness, heaviness, warmth and coldness (of things), the sense of touch (tvac) was created On it grow the hairs (the sense-organ) and trees (the presiding deities). The vital air having obtained the quality of touch by means of the skin, covers it internally and externally.

devanagari: हस्तौ रुरुहतुः तस्य नाना कर्म चिकीर्षया । तयोस्तु बलमिन्द्रश्च आदानं उभयाश्रयम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: hastau ruruhatuḥ tasya nānā karma cikīrṣayā tayostu balamindraśca ādānaṃ ubhayāśrayam
translation: Through his desire to do various activities, hands grew to him, (i.e. he developed hands). In them, the power (to work) as sense, and Indra (as the presiding deity) were produced. The action of receiving depends on them both.

devanagari: गतिं जिगीषतः पादौ रुरुहातेऽभिकामिकाम् । पद्‍भ्यां यज्ञः स्वयं हव्यं कर्मभिः क्रियते नृभिः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: gatiṃ jigīṣataḥ pādau ruruhāte pad‍bhyāṃ yajñaḥ svayaṃ havyaṃ karmabhiḥ kriyate nṛbhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ ji ], [ablative single from √ ji ], [genitive single from √ ji ]
[ablative single from √ ji ], [genitive single from √ ji ]
[nominative single from √ hu class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ hu class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ hū class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hū class 1 verb]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: When he wished to go to a desired destination, two legs (feet) grew out (from his body). Along with them, Viṣṇu the sacrifice incarnate became the presiding deity. The collection of materials for sacrifice was made by men by the action of going [The organ of motion whereby men can go for collection of sacrificial material was produced].

devanagari: निरभिद्यत शिश्नो वै प्रजानन्द अमृतार्थिनः । उपस्थ आसीत् कामानां प्रियं तद् उभयाश्रयम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: nirabhidyata śiśno vai prajānanda amṛtārthinaḥ upastha āsīt kāmānāṃ priyaṃ tad ubhayāśrayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: When he wanted progeny, pleasure and heaven (or immortality by one’s continuation of race through offspring), the place of generative organ[8], the organ of generation(along- with Prajāpati as the presiding deity) appeared. Sexual pleasure depends on them both (the deity and the sense-organ).
purport:
Footnote [8]:
śiṣṇa here stands for both male and female organs.

devanagari: उत्सिसृक्षोः धातुमलं निरभिद्यत वै गुदम् । ततः पायुस्ततो मित्र उत्सर्ग उभयाश्रयः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: utsisṛkṣoḥ dhātumalaṃ nirabhidyata vai gudam tataḥ pāyustato mitra utsarga ubhayāśrayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When he wanted to discharge the impurities of the food etc. (eaten by him), the anus appeared along with the organ discharging excrement, and with Mitra as the presiding deity. On these two depends the function of discharging the the excrement.

devanagari: आसिसृप्सोः पुरः पुर्या नाभिद्वारं अपानतः । तत्र अपानः ततो मृत्युः पृथक्त्वं उभयाश्रयम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: āsisṛpsoḥ puraḥ puryā nābhidvāraṃ apānataḥ tatra apānaḥ tato mṛtyuḥ pṛthaktvaṃ ubhayāśrayam
translation: When he desired to move out (completely) from one body to another, the outlet—the navel—was formed with apāna (as the sense organ) and mṛtyu (the goddess of death as the presiding deity) was formed. On these two depends the separation (departing) from the body.

devanagari: आदित्सोः अन्नपानानां आसन् कुक्ष्यन्न नाडयः । नद्यः समुद्राश्च तयोः तुष्टिः पुष्टिः तदाश्रये ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: āditsoḥ annapānānāṃ āsan kukṣyanna nāḍayaḥ nadyaḥ samudrāśca tayoḥ tuṣṭiḥ puṣṭiḥ tadāśraye
translation: To him who became desirous for the intake of food and drink, stomach, intestines and veins (blood vessels) appeared with rivers and seas (as presiding deities). The satisfaction and the nourishment (of body) depend on them both (the organs and the deities).

devanagari: निदिध्यासोः आत्ममायां हृदयं निरभिद्यत । ततो मनः ततश्चंद्रः सङ्कल्पः काम एव च ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: nididhyāsoḥ ātmamāyāṃ hṛdayaṃ nirabhidyata tato manaḥ tataścaṃdraḥ saṅkalpaḥ kāma eva ca
translation: When he wished to meditate on the Māyā of the Soul (ātman), the heart was produced. Then the mind (as the inner sense organ), the Moon (as the deity) and the functions of thinking and desiring (were produced).[9]
purport:
Footnote [9]:
This is obviously based on the Aitareya Upaniṣad: Khaṇḍas 1 and 2. The mutual relations of the physical member of the sense organ, the organ of the sense, and the presiding deity as given in the Ait. Up. are tabulated below, which may be compared with the above description in the Bhāgavata Purāṇa
Sense-organ.

devanagari: त्वक् चर्म मांस रुधिर मेदो मज्जास्थि धातवः । भूम्यप्तेजोमयाः सप्त प्राणो व्योमाम्बु वायुभिः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tvak carma māṃsa rudhira medo majjāsthi dhātavaḥ bhūmyaptejomayāḥ sapta prāṇo vyomāmbu vāyubhiḥ
translation: The seven essential ingredients of the body[10] are the inner skin and the outer skin, flesh, blood, fat, marrow anḍ bones which were created respectively from the earth, water, fire, prāṇa, ether, water and the air.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
dhātu—The usual list is rasāsṛṅ-māṃsa-medo'sthimajjā-śukrāṇi dhātavaḥ /—ASD. 524.
But here in Bhāgavata Purāṇa rasa and śukra are omitted and the cognitive organ īvac (and carman) are substituted for them.

devanagari: गुणात्मकान् इंद्रियाणि भूतादि प्रभवा गुणाः । मनः सर्व विकारात्मा बुद्धिर्विज्ञानरूपिणी ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇātmakān iṃdriyāṇi bhūtādi prabhavā guṇāḥ manaḥ sarva vikārātmā buddhirvijñānarūpiṇī
translation: The organs of the senses have for their soul (i.e. are essentially related to) their objects (such as sound, touch etc.). Guṇas proceed from ahaṃkāra (ego). The mind consists of all vikāras (emotions, perturbations etc.). And Intelligence is characterised by the knowledge of the things as they are[11].
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Intelligence is a form of mahat-tattva—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: एतद् भगवतो रूपं स्थूलं ते व्याहृतं मया । मह्यादिभिश्च आवरणैः अष्टभिः बहिरावृतम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: etad bhagavato rūpaṃ sthūlaṃ te vyāhṛtaṃ mayā mahyādibhiśca āvaraṇaiḥ aṣṭabhiḥ bahirāvṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [ablative single from √ mah ]
[ablative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [ablative single from √ mah ]

translation: I have expounded to you the gross form of the Lord. It is covered from outside, with eight sheaths, of which the earth is the first.[12]
purport:
Footnote [12]:
The remaining are of water, fire, air, ether, Mahat, ahaṃkāra and Prakṛti.
According to Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, here ends the description of the gross body (Virāṭ Śarīra) of the Lord.

devanagari: अतः परं सूक्ष्मतमं अव्यक्तं निर्विशेषणम् । अनादिमध्यनिधनं नित्यं वाङ् मनसः परम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: ataḥ paraṃ sūkṣmatamaṃ avyaktaṃ nirviśeṣaṇam anādimadhyanidhanaṃ nityaṃ vāṅ manasaḥ param
translation: [13] Beyond this (gross—Virāṭ form of the Lord) is the subtlest, unmanifest, attributeless, (form) which has no beginning, middle or end (i.e. not subject to creation, subsistence and destruction). It is eternal and beyond the reach of words and mind.
purport:
Footnote [13]:
According to Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Siddhāntapradīpa and Subodhinī this is description of the subtle body (samaṣṭi-liṅga śarīra) of God, but Bhāgavata Candrikā interprets this as referring to mukta-jīvas, i.e. the individual souls in liberated condition, while Padaratnāvalī takes it as applying to the transcendental form of the Supreme Lord Hari.

devanagari: अमुनी भगवद् रूपे मया ते ह्यनुवर्णिते । उभे अपि न गृह्णन्ति मायासृष्टे विपश्चितः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: amunī bhagavad rūpe mayā te hyanuvarṇite ubhe api na gṛhṇanti māyāsṛṣṭe vipaścitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation: I have described to you these two forms of the Lord. But sages[14] (learned men) do not take (accept) either of them as they are created by Māyā.
purport:
Footnote [14]:
Padaratnāvalī reads avipaścita [a-vipaścitaḥ], i.e. people who are weak in knowledge. Such persons cannot understand the above described two forms which are created by God’s will and Prakṛti.

devanagari: स वाच्य वाचकतया भगवान् ब्रह्मरूपधृक् । नामरूपक्रिया धत्ते सकर्माकर्मकः परः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vācya vācakatayā bhagavān brahmarūpadhṛk nāmarūpakriyā dhatte sakarmākarmakaḥ paraḥ
translation: The Lord assumes the form of Brahmā and takes names, forms and actions, himself being both the things designated and the word denoting it. He is both the doer of actions, (through Māyā, but really) non-doer[15]. He is different and beyond the both.
purport:
Footnote [15]:
akarmakaḥ [akarmaka]—As God’s will works (not he), he is not bound by the actions—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: प्रजापतीन् मनून् देवान् ऋषीन् पितृगणान् पृथक् । सिद्धचारणगन्धर्वान् विद्याध्रासुर गुह्यकान् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāpatīn manūn devān ṛṣīn pitṛgaṇān pṛthak siddhacāraṇagandharvān vidyādhrāsura guhyakān
translation: He assumes (the names, forms and actions of) Prajāpatis, Manus, gods, sages, manes (Pitṛs), siddhas, semi-divine beings like Cāraṇas, Gandharvas, Vidyādharas; Asuras, Yakṣas, Kinnaras, celestial damsels, Nāgas, serpents, Kimpuruṣas (the same as Kinnaras), men, Mātṛs (such as Brāhmī, Māheśvarī etc.), evil beings like demons, Pretas,[16] Piśācas, Bhūtas (goblins), Vināyakas, imps like Kūṣmāṇḍa, Unmāda (like Kālakarṇa), Vetāla, Yātudhāna (a kind of demon), planets, birds, deer, beasts, trees, mountains and reptiles.
purport:
Footnote [16]:
Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa applies the term preta to those who met an accidental death.

devanagari: किन्नराप्सरसो नागान् सर्पान् किम्पुरुषोरगान् । मातृरक्षःपिशाचांश्च प्रेतभूतविनायकान् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kinnarāpsaraso nāgān sarpān kimpuruṣoragān mātṛrakṣaḥpiśācāṃśca pretabhūtavināyakān
translation: He assumes (the names, forms and actions of) Prajāpatis, Manus, gods, sages, manes (Pitṛs), siddhas, semi-divine beings like Cāraṇas, Gandharvas, Vidyādharas; Asuras, Yakṣas, Kinnaras, celestial damsels, Nāgas, serpents, Kimpuruṣas (the same as Kinnaras), men, Mātṛs (such as Brāhmī, Māheśvarī etc.), evil beings like demons, Pretas,[16] Piśācas, Bhūtas (goblins), Vināyakas, imps like Kūṣmāṇḍa, Unmāda (like Kālakarṇa), Vetāla, Yātudhāna (a kind of demon), planets, birds, deer, beasts, trees, mountains and reptiles.
purport:
Footnote [16]:
Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa applies the term preta to those who met an accidental death.

devanagari: कूष्माण्दोन्माद वेतालान् यातुधानान् ग्रहानपि । खगान् मृगान् पशून् वृक्षान् गिरीन् नृप सरीसृपान् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kūṣmāṇdonmāda vetālān yātudhānān grahānapi khagān mṛgān paśūn vṛkṣān girīn nṛpa sarīsṛpān
translation: He assumes (the names, forms and actions of) Prajāpatis, Manus, gods, sages, manes (Pitṛs), siddhas, semi-divine beings like Cāraṇas, Gandharvas, Vidyādharas; Asuras, Yakṣas, Kinnaras, celestial damsels, Nāgas, serpents, Kimpuruṣas (the same as Kinnaras), men, Mātṛs (such as Brāhmī, Māheśvarī etc.), evil beings like demons, Pretas,[16] Piśācas, Bhūtas (goblins), Vināyakas, imps like Kūṣmāṇḍa, Unmāda (like Kālakarṇa), Vetāla, Yātudhāna (a kind of demon), planets, birds, deer, beasts, trees, mountains and reptiles.
purport:
Footnote [16]:
Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa applies the term preta to those who met an accidental death.

devanagari: द्विविधाश्चतुर्विधा येऽन्ये जल स्थल वनौकसः । कुशलाअकुशला मिश्राः कर्मणां गतयस्त्विमाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: dvividhāścaturvidhā ye kuśalāakuśalā miśrāḥ karmaṇāṃ gatayastvimāḥ
translation: He creates all beings which fall into two categories (moving and stationary), and those divided into four classes according to their process of birth (viz. born out of the egg, the womb, sweat and seeds): and those classified in three as per their habitat (viz. those which live on the land, in the water and in the air). These are the fruits of the actions—merit, demerit and a mixture of the two.[17]
purport:
Footnote [17]:
This is a reply to Parīkṣit’s query yāvatyaḥ karmag atoyo jādṛśīr dvija-sattāma (Bhāgavata Purāṇa II.8.156).

devanagari: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति तिस्रः सुरनृनारकाः । तत्राप्येकैकशो राजन् भिद्यन्ते गतयस्त्रिधा । यद् एकैकतरोऽन्याभ्यां स्वभाव उपहन्यते ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvaṃ rajastama iti tisraḥ suranṛnārakāḥ tatrāpyekaikaśo rājan bhidyante gatayastridhā yad ekaikataro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sur class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The consequences of the actions are three according to the three attributes, sattva, rajas and tamas, and (according to the predominance of the attribute) beings become residents in the heaven, this world and the hell. Oh king, when one out of these attributes is dominated by the other two, each of these courses are then subdivided in three varieties.

devanagari: स एवेदं जगद्धाता भगवान् धर्मरूपधृक् । पुष्णाति स्थापयन् विश्वं तिर्यङ्नरसुरादिभिः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa evedaṃ jagaddhātā bhagavān dharmarūpadhṛk puṣṇāti sthāpayan viśvaṃ tiryaṅnarasurādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā ], [vocative single from √ sthā ]

translation: [18] This very glorious Lord who created the universe, takes the form of Dharma (upholder of the order) and protects and nourishes the universe, by taking incarnations in beasts, men, gods.
purport:
Footnote [18]:
Having described creation of the universe in the capacity of Brahman, the author describes the protection aspect in the capacity of Viṣṇu in this verse, and destructive aspect of Rudra in the next verse.

devanagari: ततः कालाग्निरुद्रात्मा यत्सृष्टं इदमात्मनः । सं नियच्छति तत्काले घनानीकं इवानिलः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kālāgnirudrātmā yatsṛṣṭaṃ idamātmanaḥ saṃ niyacchati tatkāle ghanānīkaṃ ivānilaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: Then in due course, he, assuming the form of the Time of the world-destruction, the world-consuming Fire and Rudra[19], like a wind dispersing a host of clouds, destroys the world that is created by him.
purport:
Footnote [19]:
Bālaprabodhini Dīpanī—Radharaman Gosvami.

devanagari: इत्थं भावेन कथितो भगवान् भगवत्तमः । न इत्थं भावेन हि परं द्रष्टुं अर्हन्ति सूरयः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ bhāvena kathito bhagavān bhagavattamaḥ na itthaṃ bhāvena hi paraṃ draṣṭuṃ arhanti sūrayaḥ
translation: [20] The divine lord of unimaginable power, glory etc. has been thus described (in his capacity of creator, protector and destroyer). But wise men should not think him to be of this much description.
purport:
Footnote [20]:
The venerable lord (Viṣṇu) is superior to other great gods (like Brahmā and Rudra). He has been described as quite different from others, and free from all defects (despite his actions of creation, destruction etc.). Wise men should look upon him as distinct from others (and not identical with others)—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: नास्य कर्मणि जन्मादौ परस्य अनुविधीयते । कर्तृत्वप्रतिषेधार्थं माययारोपितं हि तत् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: nāsya karmaṇi janmādau parasya anuvidhīyate kartṛtvapratiṣedhārthaṃ māyayāropitaṃ hi tat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: Para Brahman is not a doer in the activity pertaining to creation (protection or destruction of the universe). It is for the sake of refuting his relation as agent that the description is given. It is imposed on him by Māyā.

devanagari: अयं तु ब्रह्मणः कल्पः सविकल्प उदाहृतः । विधिः साधारणो यत्र सर्गाः प्राकृतवैकृताः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ tu brahmaṇaḥ kalpaḥ savikalpa udāhṛtaḥ vidhiḥ sādhāraṇo yatra sargāḥ prākṛtavaikṛtāḥ
translation: [21] Oh king! this kalpa-period of god Brahmā along with its subdivisions (vikalpas) has been described to you. In this kalpa, (subtle) creations from prakṛti or mahat called Prākṛtikas and (gross) creations known as Vaikṛtika take place. Such types of creations are common to all kalpas[22].
purport:
Footnote [21]:
This is the reply to the question ‘yāvān kalpo vikalpo vā’ etc. in Bhāgavata Purāṇa 2.8.12.

Footnote [22]:
Kalpa.—a day of Brahmā or 1000 yugas or 432 million years of mortal beings. Kramasandarbha. quotes from the Skanda Purāṇa (Prabhāsa Khaṇḍa) the list of 30 Kalpas. The present is the Śvetavarāha Kalpa.

devanagari: परिमाणं च कालस्य कल्पलक्षण विग्रहम् । यथा पुरस्ताद् व्याख्यास्ये पाद्मं कल्पमथो श्रृणु ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: parimāṇaṃ ca kālasya kalpalakṣaṇa vigraham yathā purastād vyākhyāsye pādmaṃ kalpamatho śrṛṇu
translation: I shall fully explain to you later the measure of time (both gross and subtle), the definition of kalpa and its division. Listen now to Pādma Kalpa.

devanagari: शौनक उवाच । यदाह नो भवान् सूत क्षत्ता भागवतोत्तमः । चचार तीर्थानि भुवः त्यक्त्वा बंधून् सुदुस्त्यजान् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: śaunaka uvāca yadāha no bhavān sūta kṣattā bhāgavatottamaḥ cacāra tīrthāni bhuvaḥ tyaktvā baṃdhūn sudustyajān

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śaunaka said:
Oh Sūta! you told us that Vidura, the best of the devotees of the Lord, travelled to the holy places on the earth after leaving his relatives who are abandoned with great difficulty.

devanagari: क्षत्तुः कौशारवेः तस्य संवादोऽध्यात्मसंश्रितः । यद्वा स भगवान् तस्मै पृष्टः तत्त्वं उवाच ह ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣattuḥ kauśāraveḥ tasya saṃvādo yadvā sa bhagavān tasmai pṛṣṭaḥ tattvaṃ uvāca ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: At what place did his discussion with Maitreya (Kauṣārava) on topics pertaining to the Soul take place? What did the revered Vidura ask him? What was his (Maitreya’s) discourse (in reply)?

devanagari: ब्रूहि नः तद् इदं सौम्य विदुरस्य विचेष्टितम् । बन्धुत्याग निमित्तं च यथैव आगतवान् पुनः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: brūhi naḥ tad idaṃ saumya vidurasya viceṣṭitam bandhutyāga nimittaṃ ca yathaiva āgatavān punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 1 verb]

translation:
Oh gentle Sūta! Please tell us all about it, and about Vidura’s activities, the cause of his leaving his brother and his return as well.
Please listen.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । राज्ञा परीक्षिता पृष्टो यद् अवोचत् महामुनिः । तद्वोऽभिधास्ये श्रृणुत राज्ञः प्रश्नानुसारतः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca rājñā parīkṣitā pṛṣṭo yad avocat mahāmuniḥ tadvo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Sūta said:
I shall narrate to you in the order of questions put to the great sage by Parīkṣit and what discourses he gave on these questions.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां संहितायां । द्वितीयस्कंधे दशमोऽध्यायः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmadbhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ dvitīyaskaṃdhe daśamo
